In this episode, we share our tax filing journey from student days to becoming working professionals, along with the various emotions and challenges faced when dealing with taxes. Additionally, they'll introduce some surprisingly strange taxes from history, as well as tax-related English idioms and phrases that will help us discuss this topic more naturally and fluently.

title: Souvenir Stories: Bonds Forged on the Krugerwildtuin Safari
summary: Fluent Fiction - Afrikaans: Souvenir Stories: Bonds Forged on the Krugerwildtuin Safari  Find the full episode transcript, vocabulary words, and more: fluentfiction.com/af/episode/2025-05-12-22-34-02-af  Story Transcript: Af: Die son het laag gesak oor die uitgestrekte savanne van die Krugerwildtuin. En: The sun had sunk low over the vast savanna of the Krugerwildtuin. Af: Dit was herfs, en die lug was koel en vars. En: It was autumn, and the air was cool and fresh. Af: Elsa, Johan, en Marlene het hul weg gemaak na die geskenkwinkel in die park. En: Elsa, Johan, and Marlene made their way to the gift shop in the park. Af: Dit was tyd om 'n spesiale aandenking vir Johan te vind. En: It was time to find a special souvenir for Johan. Af: Die winkel was vol skatte. En: The shop was full of treasures. Af: Rakke was oorvol met kleurvolle voorwerpe. En: Shelves were overflowing with colorful items. Af: Van klein gedenkwaardighede tot imposante handwerk van die plaaslike gemeenskappe. En: From small memorabilia to impressive crafts from the local communities. Af: Die geur van die wildernis het die lug gevul terwyl helder sonskyn deur die groot vensters geskyn het. En: The scent of the wilderness filled the air as bright sunshine streamed through the large windows. Af: Johan was oorweldig. En: Johan was overwhelmed. Af: "Daar is soveel om van te kies," het hy gesГӘ, terwyl hy na 'n ry met gesnyde figure gewys het. En: "There is so much to choose from," he said, pointing to a row with carved figures. Af: Marlene, vol wysheid, het haar hand op sy skouer gesit. En: Marlene, full of wisdom, placed her hand on his shoulder. Af: вҖңKyk na die verhale agter die voorwerpe,вҖқ het sy gesГӘ. En: "Look at the stories behind the items," she said. Af: вҖңElke stuk het 'n storie.вҖқ En: вҖңEach piece has a story.вҖқ Af: Elsa het besluit om Johan te help. En: Elsa decided to help Johan. Af: Sy het geglimlag en gesГӘ: вҖңKom ons kyk na die handgemaakte items wat die plaaslike gemeenskappe ondersteun.вҖқ En: She smiled and said, вҖңLetвҖҷs look at the handmade items that support the local communities.вҖқ Af: Hulle het die tipiese suigstokkies en T-hemde vermy en na allerhande kreatiewe werke gekyk. En: They avoided the typical lollipops and T-shirts and looked at all sorts of creative works. Af: Hulle het by 'n hoek met gerecycleerde materiaal gestop. En: They stopped at a corner with recycled materials. Af: Daar was pragtige hangertjies en skilderye gemaak van blikkies en drade. En: There were beautiful pendants and paintings made of cans and wires. Af: Elsa het 'n pragtige armband opgetel. En: Elsa picked up a beautiful bracelet. Af: вҖңHierdie is deel van 'n projek wat wildbewaring ondersteun,вҖқ het sy verduidelik. En: вҖңThis is part of a project that supports wildlife conservation,вҖқ she explained. Af: вҖңDit help om werk te skep en die omgewing te beskerm.вҖқ En: вҖңIt helps create jobs and protect the environment.вҖқ Af: Johan het na 'n tradisionele gesnyde leeufiguur gekyk. En: Johan looked at a traditional carved lion figure. Af: Dit was indrukwekkend, maar sy oog het teruggekeer na die eenvoudige, maar betekenisvolle armband. En: It was impressive, but his eye returned to the simple yet meaningful bracelet. Af: Hy kon die passie in Elsa se stem hoor en die waarheid in Marlene se treurige glimlag sien. En: He could hear the passion in Elsa's voice and see the truth in Marlene's wistful smile. Af: "In die armband sien ek meer as net skoonheid," het Johan gesГӘ. En: "In the bracelet, I see more than just beauty," Johan said. Af: вҖңEk sien 'n storie van hoop en beskerming, iets wat ek huis toe kan neem en elke dag onthou.вҖқ En: вҖңI see a story of hope and protection, something I can take home and remember every day.вҖқ Af: Marlene het goedkeurend geknik. En: Marlene nodded approvingly. Af: вҖңJy het 'n goeie keuse gemaak,вҖқ het sy gesГӘ. En: вҖңYou made a good choice,вҖқ she said. Af: Johan het die armband gekoop en na Elsa gekyk met dankbaarheid in sy oГ«. En: Johan bought the bracelet and looked at Elsa with gratitude in his eyes. Af: вҖңDankie dat jy my gewys het wat regtig saak maak.вҖқ En: вҖңThank you for showing me what really matters.вҖқ Af: Toe hulle die winkel verlaat, het Johan se hart geswel van waardering. En: As they left the shop, Johan's heart swelled with appreciation. Af: Hy het besef dat sy reis na Suid-Afrika meer was as net 'n besoek; dit was 'n verbinding met die aarde en met Elsa. En: He realized that his journey to Suid-Afrika was more than just a visit; it was a connection with the earth and with Elsa. Af: Die band tussen hulle het sterker geword, soos die riviere wat deur die dorre landskap vloei en nuwe lewe bring. En: The bond between them had grown stronger, like the rivers that flow through the barren landscape, bringing new life. Af: Elsa het teruggekyk oor haar skouer en gesГӘ, вҖңElke dag is 'n geleentheid om 'n verskil te maak.вҖқ En: Elsa looked back over her shoulder and said, вҖңEvery day is an opportunity to make a difference.вҖқ Af: En daarmee het hulle verder deur die Krugerwildtuin gestap, met die wete dat hulle, net soos die armband, deel was van iets veel groter. En: And with that, they continued their walk through the Krugerwildtuin, knowing that they, just like the bracelet, were part of something much greater.  Vocabulary Words: sunk: gesakvast: uitgestrektefresh: varssouvenir: aandenkingtreasures: skatteoverflowing: oorvolmemorabilia: gedenkwaardighedewilderness: wildernisstreamed: geskynoverwhelmed: oorweldigcarved: gesnydewisdom: wysheidhandmade: handgemaaktetypical: tipiesecreative: kreatiewerecycled: gerecycleerdependants: hangertjiesconservation: bewaringtraditional: tradisioneleimpressive: indrukwekkendpassion: passiewistful: treurigeappreciation: waarderingconnection: verbindingbarren: dorreopportunity: geleentheiddifference: verskillandscape: landskapbond: bandflow: vloei

title: 192. ВҝComprar o adoptar mascotas?
podcast_name: ВЎCuГ©ntame! | Learn Spanish with Comprehensible Input
summary: NEWS: #EQUIPOGATO HAS WON THE CONTEST! рҹҺҷпёҸEpisode 192: Adopt or Buy a Pet? In this episode, Marta invites listeners to reflect on the ways we choose to bring a pet into our lives. Is it better to adopt or to buy a pet? Why do we choose one over the other, and what impact does that decision have? Marta shares personal experiencesвҖ”both buying and later adopting dogsвҖ”and challenges some common misconceptions about pet adoption, breeding, and animal health. title: E73  Your Guide to American Prom Season: Building English Vocabulary
summary: рҹҺү ItвҖҷs Prom Season! In this episode of the Learn English Podcast, weвҖҷre talking about a fun and exciting part of American school life вҖ” school dances! Have you ever seen those iconic prom scenes in movies and TV shows and wondered what they're really like? We'll explore this important part of American teenage culture, focusing on the biggest dance of them all: prom, which happens every spring вҖ“ just like right now! рҹ’ғ We'll be looking closely at the specific words and phrases people use when talking about these dances. From the different types of dances throughout the school year, like Homecoming and Winter Formal, to the traditions like asking someone to prom, you'll learn essential vocabulary in a real-life context. The episode is packed with authentic vocabulary and examples that will help you understand American teenage culture. рҹ“ё Plus, we'll have some fun discussing what Hollywood gets right вҖ“ and wrong вҖ“ about these memorable occasions. Do students really vote for a prom king and queen? Are the dances always in a school gym? Do teachers really come and watch the students? We'll also recommendations for movies and TV shows that depict school dances, giving you a visual reference to complement the podcast. рҹҺ§ This episode is full of useful vocabulary, cultural insights, and real-life examples to help you build your English naturally. DonвҖҷt forget to check the episode description for the full vocabulary list. If you want to understand American culture and speak more confidently, this episode is for you! рҹ”” Follow us on social media @LearnEnglishPod and visit our website: Podcast website: https://learnenglishpod.com/ Follow us on social Media: https://linktr.ee/learnenglishpod рҹ“ҡAffiliate link for italki: https://www.italki.com/en/i/ref/GBBdbb Affiliate link for Lingq: https://www.lingq.com/?referral=msusc Episode on American Schools: https://youtu.be/zRGOPQXTXms рҹ“қ Vocabulary list: 1) Prom season: The time of year when high school prom dances typically occur. 2) Middle School: The school years between elementary and high school. 3) Homecoming: A dance held in the fall, often for alumni to visit. 4) Winter formal: A formal dance held in the winter. 5) Boutonniere: A small floral decoration worn by men on their jacket. 6) Corsage: A floral piece worn by women, often on the wrist. 7) Going stag: Attending without a date. 8) King or queen of the dance: Students elected as the most popular. 9) Pep rally: A school gathering to build enthusiasm, often before a sports event. 10) Promposal: a creative or elaborate way of asking someone to prom 11) Sadie Hawkins: A dance where girls traditionally ask boys. 12) Entire point: The main purpose

title: Let's Learn English at the Strip Mall / Shopping Plaza
podcast_name: Learn English with Bob the Canadian
summary: In this English lesson we'll go to the strip mall! In my part of the English speaking world we have what are called strip malls or shopping plazas. It is a place where all the stores are in a row with the door facing the parking lot. Come with me as I explain each location and give you a peek inside each store! In this English lesson I will help you learn the words: hardware store, grocery store, employment centre, nail salon, discount store, chiropractor, dentist's office, bar, restaurant, and many more! I hope you enjoyed this English lesson about strip malls! Note: This is the audio portion of a Youtube English lesson which you can watch right here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EKgn9Ylr5IY or by searching Youtube for, "Bob the Canadian Strip Mall" Support the show

title: EP35 Stop! Tu nвҖҷes pas trop vieille pour apprendre lвҖҷanglais!
summary: Andreann Duquet sвҖҷest longtemps posГ© la question pourquoi Г§a venait la chercher autant Г©motionnellement quand elle entendait la phrase вҖңJвҖҷsuis rendue trop vieille pour apprendre lвҖҷanglais.вҖқВ  And in April, it hit her! В  On lui a dit la mГӘme chose un jour, Г  propos de quelque chose quвҖҷelle aimait tellement faire, et de se faire dire quвҖҷelle Г©tait trop vieille pour Г§a.. Г§a lвҖҷa rendue tellement triste.. et contrariГ©e.В  Le pire cвҖҷest que cвҖҷГ©tait complГЁtement FAUX!В  Le pire cвҖҷest quвҖҷelle y a cru.. jusquвҖҷГ  ce quвҖҷelle reprenne le contrГҙle. В  Elle en parle dans cet Г©pisode 35 de podcast de LвҖҷanglais votre meilleur bagage!В  Andreann Duquet parle aussi de ce qui se passe dans ton cerveau en vieillissant et la vГ©ritГ© sur lвҖҷapprentissage dвҖҷune langue seconde Г  ton Гўge!В  Alors STOP! Non! Tu nвҖҷes pas trop vieille pour apprendre lвҖҷanglais!В  www.langlaisvotremeilleurbagage.comВ  avec Andreann Duquet

summary: з•¶йҮ‘жӣІжӯҢеҗҺеҫҗдҪіз‘©зҷјзҸҫеңЁ Uber Eats дёҠпјҲжҮүи©ІпјүйғҪй»һеҫ—еҲ°пјҢеұ…з„¶зӢӮй»һдёҖжіўпјҒ йӣ–з„¶й»һдёҚеҲ°зҷҪйҰ¬е’ҢеӨұиҗҪжІҷжҙІпјҢдҪҶйҰҷж°ҙгҖҒиҫЈжӨ’жҲ–е…¶д»–еҗғзҡ„з”Ёзҡ„йғҪй»һеҫ—еҲ°пҪһ еҝ«дёҠ Uber Eats жғіиҰҒзҡ„йғҪй»һй»һзңӢв®• https://fstry.pse.is/7jpjbe    вҖ”вҖ” д»ҘдёҠзӮә KKBOX иҲҮ Firstory Podcast е»Је‘Ҡ вҖ”вҖ”       -----------------------------------  рҹҺ§В йҖҡеӢӨеӯёиӢұиӘһ VIP е°ҲеҚҖ & рҹҡҖ з·ҡдёҠиӘІзЁӢВ   -----------------------------------  рҹ’ЎВ жғіиҰҒжӣҙиҮӘз„¶зҡ„жҸҗеҚҮиӢұиӘһеҠӣпјҹеҠ е…ҘVIPжңғе“ЎпјҢзҚІеҫ—е°Ҳеұ¬е…§е®№иҲҮе„Әжғ пјҒ з«ӢеҚіеҠ е…ҘVIPж–№жЎҲВ вҶ’https://open.firstory.me/join/15minstodayVIPиЁӮй–ұеёёиҰӢе•ҸйЎҢВ вҶ’https://15minsengcafe.pse.is/5cjptb  рҹ”Ҙ зӨҫжңғдәәж ёеҝғиӢұиӘһжңүиҒІжӣёиӘІзЁӢвҶ’https://15minsengcafe.pse.is/554esm    -------------------------------  рҹҢҺ 15Mins.Today зӣёй—ңйҖЈзөҗ  -------------------------------  рҹ’¬В дҪ зҡ„жғіжі•еҫҲйҮҚиҰҒпјҒз•ҷиЁҖеҲҶдә«В вҶ’ з•ҷиЁҖйҖЈзөҗ    Webпјҡwww.15mins.today  YouTubeпјҡhttps://15minsengcafe.pse.is/3rhuuy  е•ҶжҘӯеҗҲдҪң/иҙҠеҠ©дҫҶдҝЎпјҡ15minstoday@gmail.com  ж„ҸиҰӢеӣһиҰҶ : ask15mins@gmail.com    --------------------------------------------  рҹ“ңВ е–®йӣҶйҖҗеӯ—зЁҝ & е…§е®№ж‘ҳиҰҒпјҲж’ӯж”ҫеҷЁеӯ—ж•ёжңүйҷҗпјҢе®Ңж•ҙйҖҗеӯ—зЁҝи«ӢеҲ°е®ҳз¶ІжҹҘзңӢпјү  --------------------------------------------    Topic: A Monster Wind Turbine Is Upending an Industry    Twirling above a strip of land at the mouth of RotterdamвҖҷs harbor is a wind turbine so large it is difficult to photograph. The turning diameter of its rotor is longer than two American football fields end to end. Later models will be taller than any building on the mainland of Western Europe.    й№ҝзү№дё№жёҜеҸЈдёҖиҷ•зӢ№й•·еңҹең°дёҠпјҢжңүеә§йўЁеҠӣзҷјйӣ»ж©ҹеңЁиҪүеӢ•пјҢиҰҸжЁЎеӨ§еҲ°йӣЈд»Ҙиҝ‘и·қйӣўжӢҚж”қпјҢи‘үзүҮж—ӢиҪүзӣҙеҫ‘жҜ”е…©еә§зҫҺејҸи¶ізҗғе ҙжҺҘиө·дҫҶйӮ„й•·пјҢж—ҘеҫҢжӣҙж–°зүҲзҡ„йўЁж©ҹе°ҮжҜ”иҘҝжӯҗеӨ§йҷёдёҠд»»дҪ•е»әзү©йғҪй«ҳгҖӮ    Packed with sensors gathering data on wind speeds, electricity output and stresses on its components, the giant whirling machine in the Netherlands is a test model for a new series of giant offshore wind turbines planned by General Electric. When assembled in arrays, the wind machines have the potential to power cities, supplanting the emissions-spewing coal- or natural gas-fired plants that form the backbones of many electric systems today.    йҖҷеҖӢдҪҚж–јиҚ·иҳӯзҡ„е·ЁеһӢж—ӢиҪүж©ҹеҷЁпјҢжҳҜеҘҮз•°е…¬еҸёжүҖиҰҸз•«ж–°зі»еҲ—е·ЁеһӢйӣўеІёйўЁж©ҹзҡ„жё¬и©ҰжЁЎеһӢпјҢй…ҚеӮҷдәҶж”¶йӣҶйўЁйҖҹгҖҒйӣ»еҠӣијёеҮәеҸҠеҗ„йӣ¶д»¶жүҖеҸ—жҮүеҠӣж•ёж“ҡзҡ„ж„ҹжҮүеҷЁгҖӮйҖҷдәӣйўЁж©ҹиӢҘд»ҘйҷЈеҲ—еёғе»әпјҢе…·еӮҷзӮәеҹҺеёӮдҫӣйӣ»зҡ„жҪӣеҠӣпјҢеҸ–д»ЈеӨ§йҮҸжҺ’ж”ҫе»ўж°ЈгҖҒзө„жҲҗз•¶д»ҠиЁұеӨҡзҷјйӣ»зі»зөұйӘЁе№№зҡ„зҮғз…ӨжҲ–зҮғж°Јзҷјйӣ»е» гҖӮ    GE has yet to install one of these machines in ocean water. As a relative newcomer to the offshore wind business, the company faces questions about how quickly and efficiently it can scale up production to build and install hundreds of the turbines.    еҘҮз•°е…¬еҸёе°ҡжңӘеңЁжө·дёҠе®үиЈқжӯӨйЎһж©ҹеҷЁгҖӮеҘҮз•°еңЁйӣўеІёйўЁйӣ»жҘӯе ӘзЁұж–°е…өпјҢйқўиҮЁзҡ„е•ҸйЎҢжҳҜпјҢиғҪеӨҡеҝ«гҖҒеӨҡжңүж•Ҳең°ж“ҙеӨ§з”ҹз”ўиҰҸжЁЎпјҢд»Ҙе»әйҖ дёҰе®үиЈқж•ёзҷҫеә§йўЁж©ҹгҖӮ    But already the giant turbines have turned heads in the industry. A top executive at the worldвҖҷs leading wind farm developer called it a вҖңbit of a leapfrog over the latest technology.вҖқ And an analyst said the machineвҖҷs size and advance sales had вҖңshaken the industry.вҖқ    з„¶иҖҢйҖҷзЁ®е·ЁеһӢйўЁж©ҹе·ІеңЁжҘӯз•Ңеј•иө·иҪҹеӢ•гҖӮе…Ёзҗғй Ӯе°–йўЁе ҙй–Ӣзҷје•Ҷзҡ„дёҖдҪҚй«ҳз®ЎзЁұе…¶зӮәгҖҢи¶…и¶ҠжңҖж–°жҠҖиЎ“зҡ„дёҖж¬ЎйЈӣиәҚгҖҚгҖӮдёҖдҪҚеҲҶжһҗеё«иЎЁзӨәпјҢйҖҷж¬ҫж©ҹеҷЁзҡ„е°әеҜёиҲҮй җе”®йҮҸгҖҢж’јеӢ•дәҶж•ҙеҖӢз”ўжҘӯгҖҚгҖӮ    The prototype is the first of a generation of new machines that are about a third more powerful than the largest already in commercial service. As such, it is changing the business calculations of wind equipment makers, developers and investors.    йҖҷжҳҜж–°дё–д»ЈйўЁж©ҹзҡ„з¬¬дёҖеҖӢеҺҹеһӢж©ҹпјҢж–°дё–д»ЈйўЁж©ҹеҠҹиғҪжҜ”е·ІеңЁе•ҶжҘӯйҒӢиҪүзҡ„жңҖеӨ§йўЁж©ҹйӮ„иҰҒеј·дёүеҲҶд№ӢдёҖе·ҰеҸігҖӮеӣ жӯӨпјҢе®ғжӯЈеңЁж”№и®ҠйўЁеҠӣиЁӯеӮҷиЈҪйҖ е•ҶгҖҒй–Ӣзҷје•ҶиҲҮжҠ•иіҮдәәзҡ„зӣӨз®—гҖӮ    The GE machines will have a generating capacity that would have been almost unimaginable a decade ago. A single one will be able to turn out 13 megawatts of power, enough to light up a town of roughly 12,000 homes.    еҘҮз•°зҡ„йўЁж©ҹе°Үе…·жңүеҚҒе№ҙеүҚе№ҫд№Һз„Ўжі•жғіеғҸзҡ„зҷјйӣ»иғҪеҠӣпјҢдёҖеә§е°ұиғҪз”ўз”ҹ13зҷҫиҗ¬з“Ұйӣ»еҠӣпјҢи¶іеӨ й»һдә®зҙ„жңү1.2иҗ¬жҲ¶е®¶еәӯзҡ„е°ҸйҺ®гҖӮ    The turbine is capable of producing as much thrust as the four engines of a Boeing 747 jet, according to GE, and will be deployed at sea, where developers have learned that they can plant larger and more numerous turbines than on land to capture breezes that are stronger and more reliable.    еҘҮз•°иЎЁзӨәпјҢйҖҷзЁ®йўЁж©ҹиғҪз”ўз”ҹзӣёз•¶ж–јжіўйҹі747йЈӣж©ҹеӣӣе…·еј•ж“Һзҡ„жҺЁеҠӣпјҢе°ҮйғЁзҪІеңЁжө·дёҠпјҢй–Ӣзҷје•Ҷе·Із¶“зҹҘйҒ“еңЁжө·дёҠиғҪе®үиЈқжҜ”йҷёдёҠжӣҙеӨ§д№ҹжӣҙеӨҡзҡ„йўЁж©ҹпјҢд»ҘжҚ•жҚүжӣҙеј·д№ҹжӣҙеҸҜйқ зҡ„йўЁгҖӮ    The race to build bigger turbines has moved faster than many industry figures foresaw. GEвҖҷs Haliade-X generates almost 30 times more electricity than the first offshore machines installed off Denmark in 1991.    е»әйҖ жӣҙеӨ§йўЁж©ҹзҡ„з«¶иіҪжӯҘиӘҝжҜ”иЁұеӨҡжҘӯе…§дәәеЈ«й җжё¬зҡ„иҰҒеҝ«гҖӮеҘҮз•°зҡ„Haliade-XйўЁж©ҹпјҢзҷјйӣ»йҮҸе№ҫд№ҺжҳҜ1991е№ҙе®үиЈқж–јдё№йәҘиҝ‘жө·зҡ„з¬¬дёҖеә§йӣўеІёйўЁж©ҹзҡ„30еҖҚгҖӮ    In coming years, customers are likely to demand even bigger machines, industry executives say. On the other hand, they predict that turbines will reach a point at which greater size no longer makes economic sense.    жҘӯз•Ңй«ҳеұӨиЎЁзӨәпјҢжңӘдҫҶе№ҫе№ҙйЎ§е®ўеҫҲеҸҜиғҪиҰҒжұӮжӣҙеӨ§зҡ„ж©ҹеҷЁгҖӮеҸҰдёҖж–№йқўпјҢд»–еҖ‘й җжё¬пјҢйўЁж©ҹйҒІж—©жңғдҫҶеҲ°еҶҚеӨ§е°ұдёҚеҗҲз¶“жҝҹж•ҲзӣҠзҡ„жҷӮеҲ»гҖӮ  Source article: https://udn.com/news/story/6904/5180307  В   Next Article  В   Topic: A Scary Energy Winter Is Coming.    DonвҖҷt Blame the Greens. Every so often the tectonic geopolitical plates that hold up the world economy suddenly shift in ways that can rattle  and destabilize everything on the surface. ThatвҖҷs happening right now in the energy sphere.    жңүжҷӮеҖҷпјҢжүҝијүдё–з•Ңз¶“жҝҹзҡ„ең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»жқҝеЎҠжңғзӘҒз„¶з§»еӢ•пјҢжҠҠең°иЎЁжҜҸжЁЈжқұиҘҝжҗ–еҫ—еҳҺеҳҺдҪңйҹҝгҖӮйҖҷзЁ®дәӢзҸҫеңЁе°ұеңЁиғҪжәҗй ҳеҹҹзҷјз”ҹгҖӮ    Several forces are coming together that could make Vladimir Putin the king of Europe, enable Iran to thumb its nose at America and build an atomic bomb, and disrupt European power markets enough that the upcoming United Nations climate conference in Glasgow, Scotland, could suffer blackouts owing to too little clean energy.    жңүе№ҫиӮЎеӢўеҠӣжӯЈеңЁеҢҜиҒҡпјҢиғҪдҪҝдҝ„еңӢзёҪзөұжҷ®дәӯжҲҗзӮәжӯҗжҙІд№ӢзҺӢпјҢдҪҝдјҠжң—жңүжң¬йҢўдёҚжҠҠзҫҺеңӢж”ҫеңЁзңјиЈЎпјҢйҖІиҖҢйҖ еҮәеҺҹеӯҗеҪҲпјҢдёҰи¶ід»Ҙжү“дәӮжӯҗжҙІиғҪжәҗеёӮе ҙпјҢи®“еҚіе°Үзҷ»е ҙзҡ„иӢұеңӢиҳҮж јиҳӯж јжӢүж–Ҝе“ҘиҒҜеҗҲеңӢж°ЈеҖҷеӨ§жңғеҸҜиғҪжңғеӣ зӮәжҪ”ж·ЁиғҪжәҗеӨӘе°‘иҖҢеҒңйӣ»гҖӮ    Yes, this is a big one.  жІ’йҢҜпјҢе•ҸйЎҢеҫҲеӨ§гҖӮ    Natural gas and coal prices in Europe and Asia just hit their highest levels on record, oil prices in America hit a seven-year high and U.S. gasoline prices are up $1 a gallon from last year. If this winter is as bad as some experts predict вҖ” with some in the poor and middle classes unable to heat their homes вҖ” I fear weвҖҷll see a populist backlash to the whole climate/green movement. You can already smell that coming in Britain.    жӯҗдәһе…©жҙІзҡ„еӨ©з„¶ж°Је’Ңз…Өеғ№еүӣеүӣйҒ”еҲ°еҸІдёҠжңҖй«ҳпјҢзҫҺеңӢеҺҹжІ№еғ№ж јеүөдёғе№ҙж–°й«ҳпјҢиҖҢжұҪжІ№еғ№ж јжҜҸеҠ дҫ–жҜ”еҺ»е№ҙдёҠжјІ1зҫҺе…ғгҖӮеҰӮжһңд»Ҡе№ҙеҶ¬еӨ©и·ҹдёҖдәӣе°Ҳе®¶й җжё¬зҡ„дёҖжЁЈзіҹпјҢдёҖдәӣиІ§зӘ®е’Ңдёӯз”ўйҡҺзҙҡдәәе®¶иІ ж“”дёҚиө·еҸ–жҡ–иІ»з”ЁпјҢжҲ‘ж“”еҝғжңғеҮәзҸҫйҮқе°Қж•ҙеҖӢж°ЈеҖҷе’Ңз¶ иғҪйҒӢеӢ•зҡ„ж°‘зІ№еҸҚеҪҲгҖӮеңЁиӢұеңӢе·Із¶“еҸҜд»Ҙе—…еҲ°йҖҷеҖӢи·ЎиұЎгҖӮ    How did we get here? In truth, itвҖҷs a good-news-bad-news story.  жҲ‘еҖ‘жҖҺжңғиө°еҲ°йҖҷдёҖжӯҘпјҹе…¶еҜҰпјҢйҖҷжҳҜеҖӢеҘҪж¶ҲжҒҜеЈһж¶ҲжҒҜйғҪжңүзҡ„ж•…дәӢгҖӮ    The good news is that every major economy has signed onto reducing its carbon footprint by phasing out dirtier fuels like coal to heat homes and to power industries. The bad news is that most nations are doing it in totally uncoordinated ways, from the top down, and before the market has produced sufficient clean renewables like wind, solar and hydro.    еҘҪж¶ҲжҒҜжҳҜпјҢжҜҸеҖӢдё»иҰҒз¶“жҝҹй«”йғҪе·ІеҗҢж„ҸпјҢйҖҸйҒҺйҖҗжӯҘж·ҳжұ°з…ӨзӮӯйҖҷйЎһжҜ”ијғй«’зҡ„зҮғж–ҷзөҰе®¶еәӯеҸ–жҡ–е’ҢзөҰз”ўжҘӯдҫӣйӣ»пјҢжёӣе°‘зўіи¶іи·ЎгҖӮеЈһж¶ҲжҒҜеүҮжҳҜпјҢеӨҡж•ёеңӢе®¶йҖҷйәјеҒҡзҡ„жҷӮеҖҷе®Ңе…ЁжІ’жңүдә’зӣёеҚ”иӘҝпјҢз”ұдёҠиҖҢдёӢеҹ·иЎҢпјҢиҖҢдё”еёӮе ҙйӮ„жІ’иЈҪйҖ еҮәи¶іеӨ еӨҡзҡ„йўЁеҠӣгҖҒеӨӘйҷҪиғҪе’Ңж°ҙеҠӣзӯүжё…жҪ”иғҪжәҗгҖӮ    But how did the bad-news side of this story emerge so fast?  йҖҷеҖӢж•…дәӢзҡ„еЈһж¶ҲжҒҜйқўзӮәдҪ•йҖҷйәјеҝ«еҮәзҸҫпјҹ    Blame COVID-19. First, the pandemic erupted and signaled to every major economy that we were headed for a deep recession. This sent prices of all kinds of commodities, including oil and gas, into downward spirals.    иҰҒжҖӘж–°еҶ иӮәзӮҺгҖӮйҰ–е…ҲпјҢз–«жғ…зҲҶзҷјпјҢе°ҚжҜҸеҖӢйҮҚиҰҒз¶“жҝҹй«”иҖҢиЁҖж„Ҹе‘іжҲ‘еҖ‘жӯЈиө°еҗ‘ж·ұеәҰиЎ°йҖҖпјҢдҪҝеҺҹжІ№гҖҒеӨ©з„¶ж°Јзӯүеҗ„йЎһеӨ§е®—е•Ҷе“Ғеғ№ж јиө°и»ҹгҖӮ    This, in turn, led banks to choke off investment in new natural gas capacity and crude wells after seven years of already declining investments in these hydrocarbons because of lousy returns.    йҖҷйҖІиҖҢдҪҝйҠҖиЎҢеҒңжӯўжҠ•иіҮж“ҙе……еӨ©з„¶ж°Јз”ўиғҪе’ҢжІ№дә•гҖӮйҠҖиЎҢе·Іжёӣе°‘жҠ•иіҮйҖҷдәӣзўіж°«еҢ–еҗҲзү©йҒ”дёғе№ҙпјҢеӣ зӮәе ұй…¬зҺҮеҫҲе·®гҖӮ    As Bill Gates points out in his smart book вҖңHow to Avoid a Climate Disaster,вҖқ the only way to reach our climate targets is to shift production of all the big heavy industries, like steel, cement and automobiles, as well as how we heat our homes and power our cars, to electricity generated from clean energy. Safe and affordable nuclear power has to be part of our mix because, Gates argues, вҖңit is the only carbon-free, scalable energy source thatвҖҷs available 24 hours a day.вҖқ    е°ұеғҸ

дёҚиҝҮпјҢдҪ зҡ„еЈ°йҹідјҡиў«еӨ§е®¶еҗ¬еҲ°пјҢжүҖд»ҘжңҖеҘҪдёҚиҰҒе–қеҫ—еӨӘйҶүвҖҰвҖҰ жёёжҲҸи§„еҲҷпјҡ жңүдёӨдёӘи§’иүІеҸҜд»ҘйҖүжӢ©гҖӮ дҪ еҗ¬еҲ°жҲ‘зҡ„й—®йўҳд»ҘеҗҺпјҢиҰҒеҝ«йҖҹеҒҡеҮәеӣһзӯ”гҖӮеҲ«жӢ…еҝғпјҢиҝҷдәӣй—®йўҳйғҪеҫҲз®ҖеҚ•пјҢдҪ з”ҡиҮідёҚйңҖиҰҒжҖқиҖғгҖӮжҜ”еҰӮпјҢеҸҜиғҪжҲ‘дјҡй—®дҪ пјҡдҪ жңҖе–ңж¬ўе“ӘжҳҹжңҹеҮ пјҹ йӮЈдҪ еҸӘйңҖиҰҒе‘ҠиҜүжҲ‘дҪ жңҖе–ңж¬ўжҳҹжңҹеҮ е°ұеҸҜд»ҘдәҶпјҢиҖҢдё”дҪ дёҚйңҖиҰҒй©¬дёҠе‘ҠиҜүжҲ‘дҪ дёәд»Җд№ҲжңҖе–ңж¬ўе“ӘдёҖеӨ©пјҢеӣ дёәйҡҸеҗҺжҲ‘дјҡз»ҷдҪ ж—¶й—ҙжқҘиҜҙиҜҙзңӢдҪ дёәд»Җд№ҲжңҖе–ңж¬ўе“ӘдёҖеӨ©гҖӮ title: RP494: Rozmowa z Romanem
summary: In this episode of the Learn Real Polish podcast, I talk with Roman from Donbas. He shares his story of coming to Poland, his first difficult experiences with a job agency, and how that led him to seek better work. Roman tells us about bringing his family to Poland and how they adapted to their new life together. His story is both moving and inspiring. As always, I use simple Polish to make it easier for you to understand and learn. Premium members can read the full transcript of this episode in Polish at realpolish.pl. title: Simplified Speech #220 вҖ“ Semi-destination wedding
summary: In this Simplified Speech episode, Andrew and Indiana catch up and talk all about weddings. Indiana shares her recent experience traveling to Florida for her husbandвҖҷs cousinвҖҷs wedding, which was held in Miami. She explains what a Jewish wedding is like and describes some unique traditions, such as the chuppah, the ketubah, the breaking of a glass, and the fun dance called the hora. Andrew and Indiana also compare weddings in Canada, the United States, and Korea, talking about receptions, wedding cakes, group photos, and gift-giving traditions. How this episode will improve your English:  	Vocabulary: Learn useful words and expressions related to weddings, travel, and cultural traditions.  	Listening: Develop your listening skills by following a natural, friendly, and unscripted conversation between native English speakers.  	Speaking: Talk about wedding customs in your country on our Discord server or in a Culips small-group speaking class.  	Culture: Learn about real-life wedding experiences in different countries and cultures.  	Fluency: Level up your fluency by spending time with real, everyday English that native speakers use in daily life, with support from the study guide to help you practice. The Best Way to Learn with This Episode:  	Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. title: 189 - Do You Double Dip? Cultural Curiosities with Liz
summary: If you've been listening to this podcast for a while, you know Liz. She's one of my college buddies who's been on about 6 or 7 episodes. Every time she comes on, I get messages about how much you guys like her. I, of course, do too. She's smart, she's kind, and very well-spoken, which, of course, makes her a great guest to have.  In today's episode, we'll be discussing awkward cultural questions. We'll cover everything from double dipping to appropriate behavior at a U.S. dinner party, birthday party, and more. Now, I need to knowвҖ”do you double dip? title: #408 El arte de la conversaciГіn 2
summary: ВҝTe gusta contradecir a los demГЎs? O tal vez, Вҝte gusta que te contradigan? En general, no es buena idea contradecir a tu interlocutor, porque creas una atmГіsfera de confrontaciГіn, y eso no es deseable. Ya sabemos que no es bueno contradecir en las conversaciones, pero esta regla especifica un detalle: 'especialmente en asuntos triviales'. Un asunto trivial es un tema sin importancia.  No es inteligente corregir a alguien en un tema poco importante. Parece que quieras demostrar que eres superior, incluso en detalles mГӯnimos. Es mejor no decir nada.  Consigue el texto en mi web: Unlimitedspanish.com/408-el-arte-de-la-conversacion-2/

title: иҠӮзӣ®еҪ•еҲ¶жҺЁиҝҹпјҢзҫҺеӣҪдё»ж’ӯе®¶йҮҢиҰҒвҖңжқҖиҷ«вҖқпјҹвҖ”вҖ”иҒҠиҒҠзҫҺеӣҪе®¶еәӯзҡ„ж—Ҙеёёpest control
summary: дё»ж’ӯпјҡFloraпјҲдёӯеӣҪпјү+В SelahпјҲзҫҺеӣҪпјү йҹід№җпјҡBring It All Back дёҠж¬ЎдёӨдёӘдё»ж’ӯзәҰиҠӮзӣ®еҪ•еҲ¶ж—¶й—ҙзҡ„ж—¶еҖҷSelahзӘҒз„¶иҜҙиҰҒжҺЁиҝҹпјҢжҳҜеӣ дёәе®¶йҮҢиҰҒиҝӣиЎҢе…Ёйқўзҡ„pest controlвҖ”вҖ”йҷӨиҷ«пјҒиҝҷеҲ°еә•жҳҜжҖҺд№ҲеӣһдәӢе‘ўпјҹ з»ҸиҝҮSelahи§ЈйҮҠжҲ‘д»¬жүҚзҹҘйҒ“пјҢеҺҹжқҘеҘ№д»¬жүҖеңЁзҡ„ең°еҢәArizonaпјҲзҫҺеӣҪдәҡеҲ©жЎ‘йӮЈе·һпјүпјҢе®ҡжңҹйҷӨиҷ«е·Із»ҸжҳҜе®¶еәӯж—Ҙеёёе•ҰпјҒйӮЈд№ҲиҝҷдёҖжңҹжҲ‘д»¬е°ұжқҘиҒҠиҒҠзҫҺеӣҪе®¶еәӯзҡ„вҖңpest controlпјҲйҷӨиҷ«жңҚеҠЎпјүвҖқгҖӮ 01.В WhatВ happened to SelahвҖҷs house? PestВ controlВ is super normal in places like Arizona where it's dry and full of "little friends" you don't want inside.В That sounds cute but also terrifying.В  Selah shared that recently, she and her husband started noticing lots of little bugs in their home.В иө·еҲқпјҢиҷ«еӯҗеҸӘжңүеҮ дёӘпјҢжүҖд»Ҙд»–д»¬жІЎжңүеӨӘжӢ…еҝғгҖӮВ But then they got bigger... and multipliedВ (еҸҳеӨҡ)!В  What kinds of bugs did they actually see?В  CockroachesВ (иҹ‘иһӮ), earwigs(и јиһӢ), scorpionsВ (иқҺеӯҗ)В and termitesВ (зҷҪиҡҒ). ThatвҖҷs a whole squad (з®ҖзӣҙжҳҜиҷ«еӯҗе°ҸеҲҶйҳҹ)! They look as scary as they soundвҖ”especiallyВ scorpions and termites, the ones that destroy your house from the inside. On thatВ day,В SelahВ spottedВ (еҸ‘зҺ°)В a scorpion... and it was like half the size ofВ herВ hand!В В TheyВ got so jumpyВ (еҝғжғҠиӮүи·і)В theyВ had to pack up and crash atВ (жҠ•еҘ”) herВ parentsвҖҷ house for a few nights.В  е№ёиҝҗзҡ„жҳҜпјҢеҮ еӨ©еҗҺд»–д»¬йў„и®ўдәҶpest controlгҖӮProfessionalsВ (дё“дёҡдәәе‘ҳ)В came, sprayed everything downВ (е…Ёйқўе–·жҙ’иҚҜеүӮ), and theyВ finally felt safe enough to move back in. TheyВ ended up getting professional pest control. 02.В What isВ Pest Control? Pest control meansВ getting rid of unwanted insects or animals from your home or yard. It helps keep houses clean and safe.В е…¶е®һе°ұжҳҜйҷӨиҷ«гҖӮ Actually, pest control isnвҖҷt just aboutВ getting rid ofВ bugs after you see them. A lot of Americans do it ahead of timeВ (жҸҗеүҚ)вҖ”д№ҹе°ұжҳҜеңЁй—®йўҳеҸ‘з”ҹд№ӢеүҚе°ұйў„йҳІе®ғ! It's like bug insurance, but less paperwork. з»ҷд»–д»¬зҒӯиҷ«зҡ„е·ҘдҪңдәәе‘ҳз”ЁиӢұиҜӯжҖҺд№ҲиҜҙпјҹ ExterminatorВ (йҷӨиҷ«дәәе‘ҳ).В  They're like secret heroesвҖ”savingВ peopleВ from all the tiny unwanted guests! 03.В Why is Pest Control Common in the U.S.? In the U.S., especially places like Arizona andВ FloridaВ (зҫҺеӣҪдёңеҚ—йғЁзҡ„дҪӣзҪ—йҮҢиҫҫе·һ), the warm climate means bugs are active all yearВ (иҷ«еӯҗе…Ёе№ҙйғҪеҫҲжҙ»и·ғ). Plus, many people live in houses with yardsвҖ”more exposure to natureВ (е’ҢиҮӘз„¶жҺҘи§ҰжӣҙеӨҡ)В means more contact with pests.В иҝҷд№ҲзңӢзҡ„иҜқпјҢеҘҪеғҸжңүbugsд№ҹжҜ”иҫғеҗҲзҗҶдәҶгҖӮ иҝҷйҮҢеҫҲеӨҡжҲҝеӯҗйғҪиў«ејҖйҳ”зҡ„иҚүең°пјҲopen grasslandsпјүз”ҡиҮіжІҷжј пјҲdesert areasпјүеҢ…еӣҙзқҖгҖӮSo it's pretty normal to have some "unexpected visitorsВ (дёҚйҖҹд№Ӣе®ў)" sneaking in from nature! е…¶е®һиҝҷжҳҜе…¶дёӯдёҖдёӘж–№йқўпјҢеҸҰдёҖж–№йқўжҳҜд»–д»¬д№ҹзү№еҲ«йҮҚи§ҶжҲҝеұӢз»ҙжҠӨпјҲhouseВ maintenance--keeping everything clean, safe, and in good shapeпјүгҖӮ People want to protect their homes, especially from things like termites that can damage the structure. жүҖд»Ҙиҝҷе·Із»ҸжҳҜеҫҲеӨҡе®¶еәӯзҡ„ж—Ҙеёёд№ жғҜпјҲroutineпјүдәҶгҖӮ 04.В How Does Pest Control Work? First, you schedule an appointmentВ (йў„зәҰдёҖдёӘж—¶й—ҙ). The exterminator comes to check your home and yard for signs of pestsВ (е®іиҷ«зҡ„иҝ№иұЎ). Then they sprayВ (е–·жҙ’иҚҜеүӮ)В around the outside and sometimes inside if needed. еҰӮжһңй—®йўҳжҜ”иҫғдёҘйҮҚпјҢд»–д»¬еҸҜиғҪдјҡи®ҫstickyВ trapsпјҲзІҳжҚ•еҷЁпјүгҖӮFor major issues like termites, they might do fumigationвҖ”зҶҸи’ёеӨ„зҗҶвҖ”but thatвҖҷs less common. The costВ all depends on what kind of service you need.В It can range from $70 a month to $300 a monthВ (зәҰеҗҲдәәж°‘еёҒ500-2000е…ғ)вҖҰ it also depends on what kind of house you have and how serious the problem is. д»–д»¬еӨ§зәҰйңҖиҰҒжҜҸжңҲжҲ–жҜҸйҡ”дёҖдёӘжңҲеҒҡдёҖж¬ЎпјҢе…·дҪ“иҰҒеҸ–еҶідәҺд»–д»¬зӯҫи®ўзҡ„еҗҲеҗҢпјҲд»–д»¬йҖҡеёёдјҡе’Ңе…¬еҸёзӯҫи®ўйҷӨиҷ«еҗҲеҗҢпјүгҖӮBut most people will do it to prevent long term problems and having to worry about creatures in your house, especially if you live in places like Arizona. е…¶е®һеҰӮжһңйҷӨдёҖж¬Ўиҷ«е°ұиғҪдёҖеҠіж°ёйҖёзҡ„иҜқд№ҹжҢәеҘҪзҡ„пјҢиҮіе°‘д»ҘеҗҺеҫҲй•ҝдёҖж®өж—¶й—ҙйғҪдёҚз”ЁжӢ…еҝғиҷ«еӯҗзҡ„еӣ°жү°дәҶгҖӮ Are the chemicals they spray harmful to peopleВ (е–·жҙ’зҡ„иҚҜеүӮжҳҜеҗҰеҜ№дәәдҪ“жңүе®і)? Most of the time, they tell you to take your pets and stay out of the house for a few hours.В дҪҶдҪ еҸҜд»Ҙз•ҷеңЁе®¶йҮҢпјҢйҷӨйқһд»–д»¬иҰҒеҪ»еә•зҶҸи’ёпјҲfumigateпјүгҖӮ It kills the bugs and pests within 7-10 days but is not harmful for humans.В But it would harm pets at first, so they recommend to keep pets away for 5-6 hours.В  Just think of it as giving your pets a little vacation. 05.В Every Place Has ItsВ вҖңWildВ Neighbors(йҮҺз”ҹйӮ»еұ…пјүвҖқ It's likeВ Australians having to share their space with kangaroos and giant spiders--В scary but kinda funny!В  Every place has its own вҖңwild neighbors.вҖқ In Australia, it's kangaroos and giant spiders. In Canada, it's raccoons digging through trash cans. In Arizona, scorpions sometimes show up!В еҘҪеғҸжҜҸдёӘең°ж–№йғҪжңүиҮӘе·ұзҡ„е®іиҷ«й—®йўҳпјҲpest problemsпјүпјҒ йӮЈд№ҲеӨ§е®¶пјҢе°Өе…¶жҳҜеҚ—ж–№зҡ„жңӢеҸӢпјҢдҪ д»¬жңүжІЎжңүеңЁе®¶йҮҢйҒҮеҲ°иҝҮд»Җд№ҲеҘҮжҖӘзҡ„е°ҸеҠЁзү©жҲ–е®іиҷ«е‘ўпјҹж¬ўиҝҺз•ҷиЁҖе‘ҠиҜүжҲ‘д»¬пјҒ Share your wild neighbor stories with usвҖ”weвҖҷd love to hear them!

title: CBG Scenes 1.06 | Mit schwerem Herzen im CafГ©
summary: Mark and Thomas return with an emotional new episode of Scenes! ItвҖҷs late in the evening, and Johanna is still sitting at her table, lost in thought. SheвҖҷs usually happy to chat with Erna, but today, something is different. After some gentle encouragement, Johanna finally admits whatвҖҷs been bothering herвҖ”her close university friend Nadine is leaving for a year abroad, and itвҖҷs not just friendship that sheвҖҷs worried about. As Johanna struggles to find the right words, Erna reminds her that some things are best said before itвҖҷs too late. В Mark and Thomas highlight useful grammar and vocabulary, such as verbs that always require a dative object or the multiple meanings of machen. Begleite Johanna auf ihrem Weg вҖ“ die ideale Gelegenheit, Deutsch mit einer bewegenden Geschichte zu lernen! title: 195: ВҝSigues traduciendo en tu cabeza?
podcast_name: Easy Spanish: Learn Spanish with everyday conversations | Conversaciones del dГӯa a dГӯa para aprender espaГұol
summary: ВҝSientes que piensas demasiado en inglГ©s u otro idioma cuando intentas hablar en espaГұol? ВЎNo eres el Гәnico! A muchos estudiantes nos cuesta "pensar en espaГұol", hasta que dudamos si es posible pensar en otro idioma. En este episodio, Pau y Harry platican acerca de esta frustraciГіn universal, compartiendo sus experiencias personales y ofreciendo consejos y trucos para lidiar con este fenГіmeno. ВЎVamos!  Easy Spanish Community Al unirte a la comunidad de Easy Spanish puedes llevar tu experiencia de aprendizaje al siguiente nivel. Los miembros de nuestra Podcast Membership reciben: Vocab Helper: El vocabulario mГЎs importante de cada minuto del podcast directamente en la pantalla de tu celular Interactive transcript: Una transcripciГіn interactiva donde podrГЎs leer y escuchar el podcast al mismo tiempo, con una funciГіn de traducciГіn en tiempo real Exclusive aftershow: DespuГ©s de cada episodio, Pau y JosГ© discuten un poquito mГЎs sobre el tema desde un punto de vista un poco mГЎs personal. Discord community: Acceso a la comunidad en Discord de Easy Spanish, donde puedes hablar con los miembros de nuestro equipo y otras personas que, como tГә, se encuentran en la aventura de aprender nuestro idioma Extra content for our YouTube episodes: Hojas de ejercicios, listas de vocabulario y transcripciones de todos nuestros episodios de YouTube. Si todavГӯa no eres miembro de la comunidad de Easy Spanish, puedes unirte en easy-spanish.org/community (https://www.easy-spanish.org/community) EnvГӯanos un mensaje de audio ВЎYa puedes enviarnos mensajes de audio para que los escuchemos en el podcast! title: E170 La guerre des boulangeries
summary: Quand on entre dans une boulangerie franГ§aise, on peut avoir l'impression que rien n'a changГ© depuis des siГЁcles. La bonne odeur du pain qui cuit dans le four, les baguettes croustillantes, les viennoiseries dorГ©es, les conversations avec la boulangГЁre... Pourtant, depuis une vingtaine d'annГ©es, les boulangeries traditionnelles sont forcГ©es d'Г©voluer pour survivre Г  de nouveaux concurrents. Certains cassent les prix, d'autres proposent des pains premium produits В« Г  l'ancienne В». En face, les FranГ§ais consomment de moins en moins de baguettes. Alors, qui gagnera cette guerre des boulangeries ? Transcription de l'Г©pisode

Р§СӮРҫ СӮСӢ СҮР°СүРө РІСҒРөРіРҫ РҫРұСӢСҮРҪРҫ РІРҫСӮ СӮР°Рә РҝСҖРёРІСӢСҮРҪРҫ РҪР° РҝРҫР»СғР°РІСӮРҫРјР°СӮРө РҝРҫРәСғРҝР°РөСҲСҢ? РҹСҖР°РІРҙР°, РІСҒС‘ СҚСӮРҫ Р·Р°РәРҫРҪСҮРёР»РҫСҒСҢ РҪР° РҝСҖРҫСҲР»РҫР№ РҪРөРҙРөР»Рө, РәРҫРіРҙР° СҸ РҝРҫР»РөСӮРөР»Р° РІ РңР°СҖРҫРәРәРҫ, РҝРҫСӮРҫРјСғ СҮСӮРҫ СӮР°Рј Р·РҙРҫСҖРҫРІРҫР№ РөРҙРҫР№ Рё РҪРө РҝР°С…РҪРөСӮ. РҹРҫСҚСӮРҫРјСғ РұСғРҙСғ РіРҫРІРҫСҖРёСӮСҢ РІСҒС‘-СӮР°РәРё РҝСҖРҫ РҝРҫСҒР»РөРҙРҪРёРө РҝРҫР»СӮРҫСҖР° РјРөСҒСҸСҶР° Р·РҙРҫСҖРҫРІРҫР№ РөРҙСӢ. РӯСӮРҫ, РәР°Рә РҝСҖР°РІРёР»Рҫ, РәСғСҖРёРҪР°СҸ РіСҖСғРҙРәР° РёР»Рё, РҪР°РҝСҖРёРјРөСҖ, СҒРІРөР¶РёР№ СҒСӮРөР№Рә СӮСғРҪСҶР°. РҳР»Рё РұРөР»Р°СҸ Р·Р°РјРҫСҖРҫР¶РөРҪРҪР°СҸ СҖСӢРұР°, С„РёР»Рө СҖСӢРұСӢ. РқРҫ, РәР°Рә РҝСҖР°РІРёР»Рҫ, Сғ РјРөРҪСҸ РІРҫСӮ СӮР°РәР°СҸ РҝСҖРҫРҙСғРәСӮРҫРІР°СҸ РәРҫСҖР·РёРҪР°. РҡРҫРіРҙР° СҸ Р¶РёР»Р° РІ РӣР°СӮРІРёРё, СҸ РҫСҮРөРҪСҢ Р»СҺРұРёР»Р° РҝРҫРәСғРҝР°СӮСҢ СҒСӮРөР№РәРё. РқРёРәРёСӮР°: [2:39] РӯСӮРҫ РІРҫРҫРұСүРө Р»СғСҮСҲР°СҸ РҝСҖРёРҝСҖР°РІР° Рә Р»СҺРұСӢРј РұР»СҺРҙР°Рј. РңР°СҖРёРҪР°: [2:42] РқРө РұСғРҙСғ СҒРҝРҫСҖРёСӮСҢ. РўСӢ РёРҙС‘СҲСҢ РІ Р»СҺРұРҫР№ РјР°РіР°Р·РёРҪ РҝСҖРҫРҙСғРәСӮРҫРІСӢР№, СҮСӮРҫ РІ РңРҫСҒРәРІРө, СҮСӮРҫ РІ РӣР°СӮРІРёРё, Рё РҝСҖРҫСҒСӮРҫ РҝРҫРәСғРҝР°РөСҲСҢ СҒСӮРөР№Рә. РҹРҫРјРҪСҺ, СҮСӮРҫ РІ Р РҫСҒСҒРёРё РҫРҪ СҒСӮРҫРёР» РіРҙРө-СӮРҫ СҖСғРұР»РөР№ 500, СӮРҫ РөСҒСӮСҢ 5 РөРІСҖРҫ, РҪРҫ СҚСӮРҫ РұСӢР»Рҫ РҙРҫ РҪР°СҮР°Р»Р° РІРҫР№РҪСӢ РІ 2022 РіРҫРҙСғ. РўРҫ РөСҒСӮСҢ СҒРөР№СҮР°СҒ РҙРҫСҖРҫР¶Рө. Рҗ РІ РӣР°СӮРІРёРё СҸ РёС… РҝРҫРәСғРҝР°Р»Р° СӮРҫР¶Рө РҝСҖРёРјРөСҖРҪРҫ, РәСҒСӮР°СӮРё, Р·Р° 5-6 РөРІСҖРҫ. Р—РҙРөСҒСҢ СҸ РјРҫРіСғ РҪР°Р№СӮРё СӮР°РәРҫР№ Р¶Рө СҒСӮРөР№Рә, Р·РҙРөСҒСҢ СҚСӮРҫ РІ РӣРёСҒСҒР°РұРҫРҪРө, РҪРҫ РҫРҪ РұСғРҙРөСӮ СҒРҫРІСҒРөРј РҪРө СҒСӮРөР№Рә. РқРёРәРёСӮР°: [3:32] Рҗ СӮРҫРіРҙР° РҫРұСҠСҸСҒРҪРё СҒРәРҫСҖРөРө СҖР°Р·РҪРёСҶСғ РјРөР¶РҙСғ СӮРҫРҪРәРёРј РәСғСҒРәРҫРј РіРҫРІСҸРҙРёРҪСӢ Рё СҒСӮРөР№РәРҫРј. title: How Do I Overcome Distractions On My CELPIP Exam Day?
summary: Many test-takers find themselves frustrated when they struggle to achieve the CELPIP scores they desire, especially when it seems like others breeze through without any preparation.  This episode dives into the harsh realities of the CELPIP exam and will help you think carefully about the claims of people who "seem" to succeed effortlessly on the test, while most others have to work hard for good results.   We explore the importance of proper preparation, highlighting the skills necessary to deal with distractions and improve listening abilities effectively. By immersing ourselves in practice that simulates real test conditions, you'll be able to handle any disturbance that comes your way!    Join us as we unpack practical tips to boost your CELPIP performance. Takeaways:  It's crucial to have a solid preparation strategy that focuses on real test conditions.  Practice in noisy environments can help you improve your concentration and listening skills.  Using the 5W's method for note-taking will improve your listening comprehension significantly.  Don't fall for scams that promise high CELPIP scores without effort or preparation.  Consistent short practice sessions are far more effective than occasional long study marathons.  Remember, comparing yourself to others can lead to unnecessary stress and hinder your progress.  Links referenced in this episode: Join the CELPIP Success School with this 70% special podcast listener discount: celpipsuccess.com/listener CBC.ca  Entrepreneur Article: I Was Promoted 5 Times in 6.5 Years at LinkedIn. The Secret to My Success Was This 3-Step Meeting Prep Strategy Not the golf article I was referencing, but it's similar.

title: Told Faster: Mi salud mental
summary: I care a lot about mental health. I usually feel like I have a good grasp of my own mental health, but there are days I want to ignore the world and all my responsibilities in it. I have many activities that help boost my mental health and today I would like to share a few. This story is told in the first person using mostly the present tense. Important vocabulary in the story includes: вҖңme sientoвҖқ (I feel), вҖңsaludвҖқ (health), вҖңlevantar pesasвҖқ (lift weights), and вҖңfelicidadвҖқ (happiness). title: 167: Ricordi a mano.
podcast_name: Easy Italian: Learn Italian with real conversations | Imparare l'italiano con conversazioni reali
summary: Questa settimana vi raccontiamo dell'Italia vicina a noi, nel senso piГ№ geografico del termine, e non solo. Anche di qualche sbatdato turista che ha fatto spaventare un po' tutti con il suo "ricordo" a mano. Pronti? Si parte! Trascrizione interattiva e Vocab Helper Support Easy Italian and get interactive transcripts, live vocabulary and bonus content: easyitalian.fm/membership Note dell'episodio Italian Travel Phrases Tourists Often Get Wrong  -  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jsqj5q06elo Partiamo subito e partiamo per un giro. Ma arriviamo subito! Un viaggio lungo... venti minuti!  Qualche giorno fa Matteo Katie e Antonella (la videomaker che sta collaborando con Easy Italian e Joyoflanguages) sono andati a Monza, la cittГ  di Mary! Ma cosa hanno fatto? Cosa hanno visto? E com'ГЁ Monza? https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monza https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/DuomodiMonza La corona ferrea: https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corona_ferrea La monaca di Monza https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/MonacadiMonza I promessi sposi https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ipromessisposi Matteo ha usato un termine "a posteriori".  Cosa significa? Le locuzioni latine a priori e a posteriori, che tradotte letteralmente, significano "da ciГІ che ГЁ prima" e "da ciГІ che viene dopo", sono riscontrabili nella forma latina per la prima volta nei commentatori di Aristotele ad indicare una conoscenza che proviene da ciГІ che giГ  ГЁ prima (deduzione) diversa dal sapere che si raggiunge dopo aver fatto esperienza (induzione). A priori in filosofia ГЁ un concetto che si riferisce a tutto ciГІ che si puГІ conoscere indipendentemente dall'esperienza ed ГЁ quindi opposto a a posteriori, che indica una conoscenza fondata su dati sensibili desunti dall'esperienza[1]. https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apriorieaposteriori Continuiamo il giro? Andiamo in Sicilia! Cosa ГЁ successo all'aereoporto di Palermo? https://www.open.online/2025/04/24/palermo-turista-francese-bomba-a-mano-souvenir/ Dopo tutto questo girare ГЁ il caso di fare una pausa. Ci fermiamo all'Autogrill? Questa volta no, basta cibo da fastfood! Adesso anche in strada si mangia stellati. Con ALT stazione del gusto in Italia c'ГЁ qualcuno che sta cercando di cambiare un po' le abitudini degli italiani viaggiatori. https://www.enilive.it/offerta/alt-stazione-del-gusto?gadsource=1&gbraid=0AAAAAD1OBJyHeiHNkWpiaseUoLhFCoWcO&gclid=Cj0KCQjw2tHABhCiARIsANZzDWoL5oPANUvXZ2MUSC52pjrdAb5PF9ZZIySPj2LuMlS0rDllzesXUaAhNfEALwwcB&gclsrc=aw.ds Cosa ne pensate? Trascrizione Raffaele: [0:23] Buongiorno Matteo! Matteo: [0:25] Buongiorno, come va? Raffaele: [0:27] Tutto bene. A te com'ГЁ andata la settimana piГ№ breve dell'anno? Matteo: [0:33] Bene, bene. ГҲ tutto un po' strano, anche perchГ© Milano per la maggior parte di questi giorni ГЁ stata deserta. ... title: Simplified Speech #219 вҖ“ Day trips, elections, and more!
summary: In this Simplified Speech episode, Andrew and Kassy catch up and share updates about their lives. Kassy talks about taking short trips with her baby to nearby cities in Korea. She describes visiting a busy flower festival and returning to a town just to eat her favorite vegetable bread. She also talks about her upcoming move and her hope to make her new place feel more like home. Andrew talks about how voting by mail works in Canada and the recent Canadian election. He also shares a funny story about planting trees and decorating his front yard with his wife. How this episode will improve your English:  	Vocabulary: Learn everyday words and expressions that native speakers use when talking about daily life, moving, elections, and hobbies like gardening.  	Listening: Improve your listening skills by following a clear and natural conversation between native speakers.  	Speaking: Share your own life updates on our Discord server or in our Culips small-group speaking classes.  	Culture: Hear about real-life topics like voting in Canada, Korean flower festivals, and home projects.  	Fluency: Get more confident by hearing repeated examples and practicing with our study guide. title: 194: Aprendiendo a cocinar
podcast_name: Easy Spanish: Learn Spanish with everyday conversations | Conversaciones del dГӯa a dГӯa para aprender espaГұol
summary: ВҝCГіmo aprendiste a cocinar? En este podcast Carla y Pau hablan acerca de sus experiencias aprendiendo a cocinar, de sus primeras recetas, algunos accidentesвҖҰ Y de cГіmo ha evolucionado la forma en la que cocinan hasta ahora. Easy Spanish Community Al unirte a la comunidad de Easy Spanish puedes llevar tu experiencia de aprendizaje al siguiente nivel. Los miembros de nuestra Podcast Membership reciben: Vocab Helper: El vocabulario mГЎs importante de cada minuto del podcast directamente en la pantalla de tu celular Interactive transcript: Una transcripciГіn interactiva donde podrГЎs leer y escuchar el podcast al mismo tiempo, con una funciГіn de traducciГіn en tiempo real Exclusive aftershow: DespuГ©s de cada episodio, Pau y JosГ© discuten un poquito mГЎs sobre el tema desde un punto de vista un poco mГЎs personal. Discord community: Acceso a la comunidad en Discord de Easy Spanish, donde puedes hablar con los miembros de nuestro equipo y otras personas que, como tГә, se encuentran en la aventura de aprender nuestro idioma Extra content for our YouTube episodes: Hojas de ejercicios, listas de vocabulario y transcripciones de todos nuestros episodios de YouTube. Si todavГӯa no eres miembro de la comunidad de Easy Spanish, puedes unirte en easy-spanish.org/community (https://www.easy-spanish.org/community) EnvГӯanos un mensaje de audio ВЎYa puedes enviarnos mensajes de audio para que los escuchemos en el podcast! Para hacerlo tienes que ir a easyspanish.fm (https://www.easyspanish.fm) y dar clic en el botГіn amarillo que aparecerГЎ a la derecha de la pГЎgina.  Special Guest: Carla.

title: ComhrГЎ le TYM | 30 AibreГЎn 2025
summary: рҹ—Ј ComhrГЎ le TYM | 30 AibreГЎn 2025 Brought to you by the TYM Learn Irish app вҖ“ available FREE on iOS and Android рҹ”Ғ This Week on ComhrГЎ le TYM: Orlaith and Colm AI discuss these 5 news stories from last weekвҖҷs Reaction Media in Irish: MaraГӯonn Luas Speed Kills CГ© a Bhuafaidh na MГЎistrГӯ? VeidhlГӯn ag Ceant Violin at Auction Spraoi agus SlГЎinte GlasbhealaГӯ in 2025 Greenway Fun and Health in 2025 Bhuaigh na Corrs MГіr The Corrs Win Big ComhrГЎ le TYM (Conversation with TYM)! Created for learners of Irish, this podcast is brought to you by the team behind the TYM Learn Irish app, available now for free on both iOS and Android. With ComhrГЎ le TYM, youвҖҷll get to listen to natural, accessible conversations in Irish, based on real news stories from our app вҖ” all designed to help you actually use what youвҖҷre learning. рҹҺ§ What is ComhrГЎ le TYM? Each week, hosts Orlaith and Colm AI guide you through a casual conversation built around five news articles featured on the TYM app. This helps reinforce the vocabulary and phrases youвҖҷve already seen вҖ” but in a more relaxed, spoken style. ItвҖҷs the perfect next step for learners who want to hear Irish spoken in a natural, conversational way. We believe repetition and immersion are key to success in learning Irish. ThatвҖҷs why we make it easy to listen anywhere, anytime, and combine spoken Irish with structured support in our app. We break the podcast down into 5 short segments using our signature TYM Method, only on the TYM app. рҹ“ҡ What You Get on the TYM App: Irish text, tailored for A2-level learnersClear audio, spoken at 70% speed by Orlaith and Liam AISentence-by-sentence English translationsSyntax breakdowns packed with every day, useful phrases вңЁ DonвҖҷt forget вҖ“ the TYM Learn Irish app is 100% free and packed with tools to help you make real progress.
